entities.name: Francis Geisdorff
entities.context: Named in patent title notation as 'Francis Geisdorfe -patent-Illegal, 1919'

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
entities.context: Crow allottee #2336, deceased, allotted land in Crow Reservation Montana in 1907, died November 27, 1918 of influenza aged about 20

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Sr.
entities.context: Father of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., received leasing papers from Superintendent Asbury in August 1918

entities.name: Emily J. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., living at Randlett/Fort Duchesne Utah, heir to estate

entities.name: Emily M. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., also referenced as Emily M. Geisdorff in some correspondence

entities.name: Emily F. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., also referenced as Emily F. Geisdorff in some correspondence

entities.name: Emily Lucile Geisdorff
entities.context: Minor daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918, heir to estate

entities.name: Thomas R. Powers
entities.context: Purchaser of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment, of Wyola Montana and Hartman Wyoming, bid $1500 for 159.67 acres

entities.name: C. H. Asbury
entities.context: Superintendent of Crow Agency, Montana, handled the land sale proceedings

entities.name: Albert H. Kneale
entities.context: Superintendent of Uintah and Ouray Agency, Fort Duchesne Utah, assisted with proceedings involving Frederick Geisdorff Jr.

entities.name: Calvin Coolidge
entities.context: President of the United States who signed the trust patent for Francis Geisdorff on December 21, 1923

entities.name: Woodrow Wilson
entities.context: President of the United States who signed fee patent number 719146 on November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers

entities.name: John McPhaul
entities.context: Acting Assistant Commissioner of General Land Office, signed statement October 13, 1919 that no reservations or withdrawals covered the land

entities.name: Eloise Whitebear Pease
entities.context: Named on collection stamps throughout the document, collector/owner of these historical documents

entities.name: Crow Tribe of Indians
entities.context: Tribe whose reservation land was allotted; argued to be rightful owner of land upon allottee's death per trust patent terms

entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency in Montana administering Crow Reservation land sales and allotments

entities.name: Uintah and Ouray Agency
entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency at Fort Duchesne Utah, jurisdiction where Frederick Geisdorff Jr. was living

entities.context: Bank in Billings Montana holding $7.98 lease money deposited to credit of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. estate

entities.name: 159.67 acres Crow Reservation allotment
entities.context: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment of 159.67 acres sold to Thomas R. Powers for $1500

events.description: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotted 159.67 acres (S half NE, SE quarter NW, Lot 2, Section 19, T.9S R.34E) as Crow Allottee #2336; trust patent issued December 2, 1907 under Act of February 8, 1887
Francis Geisdorff allotted 648.04 acres as homestead under Act of June 4, 1920; trust patent signed by President Calvin Coolidge on December 21, 1923, patent number 227584
Thomas R. Powers made an offer to lease Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment and adjacent land owned by relatives
Government Farmer appraised Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment at $1194 in connection with Powers' lease and sale request
Petition for Sale of Land by the Original Allottee (Frederick Geisdorff Jr.) received at Crow Agency
Crow Superintendent C.H. Asbury wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Sr. sending leasing papers and requesting answer
Crow Agency offered Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment for sale on petition acknowledged before Supt. Kneale of Uintah and Ouray Agency
Acceptance of sale of allotment sent to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. for his signature
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. signed and returned acceptance form for sale of his allotment
Thomas R. Powers notified that owners of land under Crow Land Sale No. 991 accepted his bid of $1500 for 159.67 acres; balance of purchase price demanded
Superintendent acknowledged receipt of checks from Thomas R. Powers for balance of sale price ($1350 and $2.00 advertising fee); report made to Indian Bureau recommending approval
Superintendent wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. expressing doubts Indian Bureau would approve sale due to allottee being under legal age (20 years old)
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. died of influenza aged about 20, married, intestate, survived by wife Emily J. Geisdorff and minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Superintendent Kneale wrote to Crow Agency notifying that Fritz (Frederick Geisdorff Jr.) died a few days ago of influenza
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers explaining allottee was only 20 years old and offering to cancel sale and return payment
Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff wrote Crow Agency Superintendent inquiring if agency could help pay note owed by her husband before his death
Superintendent answered Emily Geisdorff's letter regretting no funds available; mentioned pending sale uncertainty due to allottee's age; promised heirship hearing
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs reviewing all matters concerning land sale, noting allottee born May 6, 1898, holding two checks from Powers totaling $1500
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Emily M. Geisdorff enclosing copy of letter to Commissioner, asking if she wished pending sale completed
Uintah and Ouray Agency notified Superintendent Asbury of birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918

Office of Indian Affairs responded to Asbury's March 24 letter stating nothing shows brother/sister want to sell allotments or widow wants sale to proceed; if heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Emily F. Geisdorff asking whether she wished sale completed to Powers for $1500 or bid rejected; enclosed authority form for her signature
Superintendent enclosed sale papers to Commissioner stating he had just received acceptance of widow of allottee
General Land Office stated no reservations or withdrawals covering the land and no reasons why patent in fee should not be issued to purchaser; signed by John McPhaul Acting Assistant Commissioner
Thomas R. Powers wrote requesting return of deposit and balance of purchase price if nothing definite done about issuance of title
Sale approved as recommended; Commissioner of General Land Office directed to issue fee patent to Thomas R. Powers
Fee patent number 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment 2336, signed by President Woodrow Wilson
Office of Indian Affairs transmitted fee patent 719146 to Superintendent Asbury at Crow Agency for delivery to Thomas R. Powers upon signed receipt
Receipt for patent issued to Thomas R. Powers as purchaser from Frederick Geisdorff Jr., allotment 2336, patent 719146
Hearing held to determine heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; testimony taken by Superintendent Kneale at Fort Duchesne
Report on heirship and recommendations transmitted to Secretary of Interior regarding estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., Crow allottee No. 2336
Heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. determined: wife Emily J. Geisdorff entitled to one half plus dower interest; daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff entitled to balance
Check 10613 for $1317.26 sent to Emily J. Geisdorff representing her share of estate proceeds; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily; $15.00 deducted for heirship fee from total of $1521.24
Report prepared analyzing the case of Frederick Geisdorff, Crow Allottee #2336, concluding land was illegally sold to non-Indian when it should have reverted to Crow Tribe upon allottee's death

financial_transactions.payee: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. estate / Crow Agency
financial_transactions.description: Purchase of 159.67 acres (Crow Land Sale No. 991, Allotment No. 2336); paid via two checks on Sheridan Banking Company: $1350.00 and $150.00 deposit previously submitted with bid
Advertising fee for Crow Land Sale No. 991, paid by check on Sheridan Banking Company dated November 2, 1918
Ten percent deposit submitted with bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991
Balance of purchase price for Crow Land Sale No. 991, check on Sheridan Banking Company
Heirship fee authorized by Indian Office, deducted from estate total of $1521.24
Check 10613 representing widow's share (one half plus dower interest) of estate proceeds from land sale
Share of estate proceeds credited to minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Bank account balance derived from lease money deposited to credit of estate in Merchants National Bank of Billings, Montana
Total amount on books at Crow Agency to credit of Frederick Geisdorff deceased, before deduction of heirship fee
Appraised value of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s 159.67 acre allotment as appraised by Government Farmer

correspondence.subject: Leasing papers for allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Answer regarding leasing of allotment
correspondence.outcome: Unknown

correspondence.subject: Acceptance of bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991
correspondence.action_requested: Remit balance of purchase price within 30 days plus advertising fee; sign and return affidavit of vendee in duplicate
correspondence.outcome: Powers paid balance via checks dated November 2, 1918

correspondence.subject: Doubts about approval of land sale due to allottee's age
correspondence.action_requested: Submit date of birth and names/addresses of all relatives; obtain statement from Superintendent Kneale recommending approval
correspondence.outcome: No reply received; allottee died November 27, 1918

correspondence.subject: Proposed purchase of Frederick Geisdorff allotment; allottee under legal age
correspondence.action_requested: Offered to cancel sale and return payment upon Powers' request
correspondence.outcome: Powers did not immediately request cancellation

correspondence.subject: Death of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: None - informational
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of allottee's death

correspondence.subject: Request for assistance paying note owed by deceased husband
correspondence.action_requested: Financial assistance to pay husband's debt
correspondence.outcome: Denied - no funds available

correspondence.subject: Response to widow's request for financial assistance
correspondence.action_requested: None
correspondence.outcome: Regretted no funds available; promised heirship hearing to determine heirs

correspondence.subject: Review of all matters concerning Frederick Geisdorff Jr. land sale
correspondence.action_requested: Instructions on how to proceed with sale; allottee born May 6, 1898; holding checks from Powers totaling $1500
correspondence.outcome: Washington responded April 26, 1919

correspondence.subject: Pending sale of deceased husband's allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Let Superintendent know whether widow wishes pending sale completed
correspondence.outcome: Widow eventually provided acceptance

correspondence.subject: Birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff
correspondence.action_requested: None - informational
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of daughter's birth date May 23, 1918

correspondence.sender: Chief Clerk, Office of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Response to March 24 letter regarding Frederick Geisdorff Jr. land sale
correspondence.action_requested: If probable heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers; if not, bid may be rejected and land readvertised after heirs determined
correspondence.outcome: Superintendent proceeded with sale papers after receiving widow's acceptance

correspondence.subject: Proposed sale of deceased husband's allotment to Thomas R. Powers
correspondence.action_requested: Sign enclosed authority form if she wishes sale to proceed; or write briefly if she does not
correspondence.outcome: Widow signed acceptance

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991 - enclosing sale papers
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale
correspondence.outcome: Sale eventually approved

correspondence.subject: Request for return of deposit and purchase price if title not being issued
correspondence.action_requested: Return of $1500 if nothing definite done about title issuance
correspondence.outcome: Superintendent wrote to Commissioner urging completion of sale

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale at early date; widow lives on another reservation and would benefit more from money than land
correspondence.outcome: Sale approved; fee patent issued November 14, 1919

correspondence.subject: Copy of letter to Indian Bureau regarding Geisdorff sale
correspondence.action_requested: None - informational
correspondence.outcome: Unknown

correspondence.sender_title: Acting Assistant Commissioner, General Land Office
correspondence.subject: Clearance for fee patent issuance
correspondence.action_requested: None - clearance statement
correspondence.outcome: No reservations or withdrawals found; patent cleared for issuance

correspondence.subject: Transmittal of fee patent 719146 to Thomas R. Powers
correspondence.action_requested: Make proper notations on records; deliver patent to person entitled when receipt signed in duplicate; forward original receipt to Washington
correspondence.outcome: Patent delivered December 19, 1919

correspondence.subject: Fee patent 719146 issued November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336 of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: None - informational notification
correspondence.outcome: Unknown

correspondence.subject: Proceeds of sale and heirship determination
correspondence.action_requested: After heirs determined, proceeds can be transferred to her Agency upon her request approved by Superintendent; minor's shares cannot be paid without special authority
correspondence.outcome: Heirs determined; payments made April 27, 1920

correspondence.subject: Heirship to estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., deceased Crow allottee No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Consideration of heirship report and recommendations
correspondence.outcome: Heirship determined; payments made to widow and daughter

correspondence.subject: Distribution of estate proceeds
correspondence.action_requested: None - payment notification
correspondence.outcome: Check 10613 for $1317.26 sent to widow; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily

legislative_actions.outcome: Dawes Act - basis for trust patent issued December 2, 1907 for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment
Act under which Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was made as amended
Act under which Francis Geisdorff's homestead allotment of 648.04 acres was made; trust patent signed December 21, 1923
Referenced in fee patent 719146 approval for Thomas R. Powers regarding water rights and reclamation provisions
Referenced as basis for Crow Tribe's ownership claim over allotted lands; argued land should have reverted to Crow Tribe upon allottee's death
